subject: Geography
title: The Volga
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Volga.

hook.text: The Volga is Europe's longest river, stretching 3,530 km through the heart of Russia.

buildup.text: Russians call it 'Mother Volga.' It drains a third of European Russia.

discovery.text: The Battle of Stalingrad was fought on its banks—a turning point of World War II.

twist.text: Soviet engineers built so many dams that the Volga is now more lake chain than river.

climax.text: The Caspian Sea, which the Volga feeds, is shrinking as climate patterns shift.

punchline.text: Russia's mother river is chained by its own dams.

quiz.question: What body of water does the Volga River feed?
